steeringCommand: Jay made this cool agent visualizer. He challenged us to come up with high taste features and PR
whatHappened: Analyzed all 5 agentviz views, discovered none tell the story of WHY. Designed Journal view, built extractor + React component + tab wiring. 723 lines. Commit: 5c1823a
levelUp: 🌱 **Feature born.** The gap between data views and narrative identified and filled in one session.

steeringCommand: I don't see the key evolutionary moments from the REPO itself. Only this session
whatHappened: Built GET /api/journal/git backend route. Parses git log, classifies commits, collapses refactoring arcs, synthesizes level-ups. 846 lines added. Commit: c171fda
levelUp: 📡 **Git history becomes the story.** Repo evolution visible without loading a session.

steeringCommand: I want to not only use the git history but the agentviz session and history
whatHappened: Merged git entries + session entries into one chronological timeline. Source badges distinguish origin. 214 lines. Commit: 1db4986
levelUp: 🔀 **Two sources, one timeline.** Git and session data unified — the full picture.

steeringCommand: Submit a test and evals to go with this. Use prior art from squad evals
whatHappened: 42 tests across 3 suites (extractor, route, component render). Tests caught 2 real bugs: single-turn milestone threshold and cross-timezone sort. Eval baselines in docs/evals.md. Commit: 248b0e4
levelUp: ✅ **Tests caught real bugs.** Milestone threshold and timezone sort fixed before shipping.

steeringCommand: Are the steering commands actual prompts?
whatHappened: Honest answer: no — git entries show commit messages, not real prompts. Added italic quotes for real human prompts, plain text for commit-derived entries. Visual honesty. Commit: 44bee71
levelUp: ✍️ **Honesty in design.** Real prompts visually distinct from inferred data.

steeringCommand: Introduced a bug. AGENTVIZ crashed. GIT_COLORS is not defined
whatHappened: Three stale variable refs missed during rename crashed the page. Fixed in minutes, but the gap was clear: no test rendered the component. Added 6 JSDOM render tests that catch ReferenceErrors. Commits: 36a00e2, 7c968c0
levelUp: 🔧 **Production crash → test coverage.** Failure created the test that prevents it.

steeringCommand: Is there a better word than Journal? Tell me five suggestions
whatHappened: Evaluated Story, Steering, Arc, Log, Narrative. 'Steering' won — it names the thing directly. Renamed across all UI, 3 test files, 2 doc files. Commit: b414d29
levelUp: 🏷️ **Naming matters.** 'Steering' says what the view shows — human decisions.

steeringCommand: The taste and quality is not close to what I want. Look at git-for-pms as the exemplar
whatHappened: Studied the git-for-pms how-was-this-built journal. Key insight: level-ups require emoji + bold headline + insight sentence — can't be keyword-matched. Rewrote synthesizers. Commit: 2acf82c
levelUp: 🎨 **Quality bar set by exemplar.** Keyword patterns replaced with the Scribe format.

steeringCommand: After the data is projected, do another pass to understand the most impactful steering commands
whatHappened: Added impact scoring: git shortstat for lines changed, turn count between steerings. Impact bars show proportional weight. Commit: f03f856
levelUp: 📊 **Impact quantified.** Lines changed + turns driven = visible steering weight.

steeringCommand: For now lets just focus on the last 10 steering commands until we can get quality right
whatHappened: Disabled auto-contribute (produced noise). Hand-curated 10 entries matching exemplar quality. Validated by reading actual rendered output — not code. Commit: 3a58b06
levelUp: 🎯 **Output is the eval.** Stopped fixing code and started reading what users see.
